lines.text: You see NeuraCard — a multimodal medical AI platform for clinical decision support.
The main problem is simple: one patient might need a brain scan, a heart tracing, an ultrasound, and a brain-wave recording. Each of these tests gets ordered, done, and read separately.
Different specialists, different machines, different schedules — no one ever sees the whole patient at once.
AI tools exist for each test type, but today's models only do one thing. They are built for a single task, and rarely leave the research lab.
Even worse, when these models give a result, they cannot explain how they got there. A verdict with no reasoning is not something a clinician can trust when a patient's life is on the line.
So the big picture never comes together, right when you need to decide. Data stays fragmented — and that means slower, less confident, less complete care.
NeuraCard solves this with one platform for every test type — Brain MRI, twelve-lead ECG, Echocardiogram, and EEG.
The workflow is simple: open a patient, upload any test, and get an instant AI reading — all in one secure workspace.
Start with Brain MRI. The platform detects the tumour and classifies it, and shows you exactly where it looked. The evidence is shown, not hidden.
Switch to the twelve-lead ECG. You get rhythm, heart rate, and heart-rate variability — every finding flagged and explained, right next to the waveform.
In the Echocardiogram module, you see the ejection fraction and chamber segmentation, measured directly from the echo loop, alongside the ultrasound frames and a three-D heart.
For EEG, the platform reviews the brain-wave recording for harmful activity — multi-channel waveforms, key indicators, and a clear summary.

Throughout, NeuraCard is built for explainability. Grad-CAM and SHAP show exactly where each model focused, so a doctor checks the reasoning, not just the answer. That is how you move from a black box to a tool you can trust.
Looking forward, the roadmap brings learned multimodal fusion, to find brain-heart links no single test can show, plus hospital trials, real-time monitoring, and full E-H-R integration.
And every test result flows into one combined PDF report, ready for the doctor to use.
